title: Eugenioides lenormandianum
taxonomy: Eugenioides lenormandianum is a flowering plant species within the family Myrtaceae, commonly known as the myrtle family. This family includes various species of trees, shrubs, and herbaceous plants found in tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. Within the Myrtaceae family, Eugenioides lenormandianum is part of the Eugenia genus, which comprises around 1,000 species of flowering plants. intro: Eugenioides lenormandianum, commonly known as the Lenormand's Eugenia, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of South America, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, headaches, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Eugenioides lenormandianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
